headline.text: L1 S3 screw fell from MM5 matrix, never recovered — X-ray rejected test piece only *2 of 5* passes.

summary: Two active holds: 1.5 pallets from L1 S3 MM4 re-screened after missing screw (replacement installed, original not found); 3 pallets from L1 S3 on appearance hold after caramel double-passed due to matrix sealing failures. L1 S2 paused to sanitize floor-dropped pouches before re-run.

holds.title: MM4 product on hold after missing screw
holds.summary: 1.5 pallets from Matrix 4 placed on hold and re-screened through X-ray after screw holding pouch-opening arms fell off at 03:20; original screw never recovered, replacement installed.
holds.status: Re-screened through X-ray by 06:19; released after no foreign material detected in finished product.

holds.title: 3 pallets on appearance hold
holds.summary: S3 product held due to poor caramel appearance after double-passing through matrices caused by sealing failures; QA not satisfied with visual quality.
holds.status: Open, pending disposition decision from production and QA leadership.

food_safety.title: MM5 screw missing, X-ray detection inconsistent
food_safety.summary: Screw from Matrix 5 pouch-opening mechanism fell during operation at 03:20; never recovered despite line stop and search; X-ray test of similar part rejected only 2 of 5 times.
food_safety.status: Replacement screw installed by 05:20; affected product re-screened and released; original screw remains unrecovered.

food_safety.title: S2 pouches fell to floor, no catch tray
food_safety.summary: Pouches fell through X-ray reject area to floor; no tray installed under machine to prevent contamination; line paused to sanitize before re-running product.
food_safety.status: Resolved on shift — pouches sanitized and successfully re-run through X-ray.

quality.title: S2 MM6 seal quality failures
quality.summary: Bad drop every ~15 bags on Matrix 6; pouches zipping before seal; held off running to prevent pouch scrap spike.
quality.status: Open, ongoing adjustments by maintenance and operations.

quality.title: S3 multiple matrix sealing failures
quality.summary: MM3 and MM5 not sealing pouches, adding to scrap; MM4 hands loose and catching on suction cups; three machines failing within single shift.
quality.status: Investigation ongoing; Caleb York and Dakota Adair troubleshooting all three machines through 2nd/3rd shift.

quality.title: S3 product deterioration from double-passing
quality.summary: 4 containers of accumulated product scrapped due to severe deterioration; product double-passed through matrices due to open bag failures.
quality.status: Scrapped; team instructed to use only shallow bins and scrap deep-bin accumulation to prevent clumping.

quality.title: Wrong tray on labeled pallet
quality.summary: Pallet found with correct item number label but incorrect tray inside; caught before reaching line.
quality.status: Resolved — pallet pulled and isolated before use.

quality.title: ML2 PTL water leak from hose joint
quality.summary: Water leaking from PTL hose joint; pressure reached 19.3 psi before venting via blue handle; maintenance addressing.
quality.status: Open, maintenance investigating joint integrity and pressure controls.

opportunities.text: Consider adding catch tray under S2 X-ray reject bin to prevent product-floor contact — based on today's sanitation pause when pouches fell without containment.
Review matrix sealing setup and preventive maintenance schedule for S3 — all three machines (MM3, MM4, MM5) showed sealing or mechanical failures within a single 8-hour window.
Add fastener torque check to S3 matrix startup checklist — two separate hardware-loss events (MM4 screw, MM5 bolt) occurred within 45 minutes on same line.
Monitor PTL hose joint integrity and pressure limits during ML2 startup checks — leak at 19.3 psi suggests fitting or pressure-relief gap.
